content.question: Does empirical evidence exist quantifying actual cost savings (or cost overruns) in Hungarian municipalities that have closed rural schools? The Finnish case suggests that cost-efficiency claims are asserted but rarely audited.
content.reduction_path: Retrospective financial audit of 5–10 Hungarian municipalities that have closed rural schools in the past 15 years, comparing budget forecasts to actual expenditure before and after closure, controlling for pupil population and transport cost shifts.
